fitScoreNote: This role is the closest structural match to what I have actually been doing: enterprise discovery workshops, C-suite AI adoption, forward-deployed technical advisory, and production LLM system design in a vertical context. The ICP for this role is the large enterprise buyer I have spent years selling to and enabling. Healthcare procurement is unusually hard. If I can get a CFO at a multi-site DSO to change how they run eligibility, I can get a VP of Engineering at a Fortune 500 to adopt Claude.

coreJobFunction.rationale: Dan's career is a near-direct match for this role's core function. He has 3+ years in forward-deployed enterprise technical advisory roles (Solutions Architect, Customer Engineer, Director of SE), documented C-suite AI adoption workshops, and a track record of driving AI adoption across non-technical organizational stakeholders. His work at Smarter Technologies: building discovery frameworks for CFO and CIO audiences, designing AI demos that abstract technical complexity, and deploying Claude to non-technical sales reps via Cowork, maps precisely to what Applied AI Architects do for enterprise industries customers.

seniority.rationale: Dan has 8 years of post-college experience with the exact trajectory this role describes: Solutions Architect to Customer Engineer to Lead TPM to Director of Sales Engineering. He owned $31.8M in pipeline end-to-end, closed 61 deals, and was promoted to VP of Solutions before the company shuttered. The only minor gap is that his Claude-advisory motion was self-directed within his own GTM org, not formally external-facing on behalf of Anthropic. That is a venue shift, not a capability gap.

technicalSkills.rationale: Dan has production evidence for nearly every technical requirement: Python (57 scripts, 943-commit Claude Code platform), Claude API (multi-agent, pre/post hooks, Cowork deployment), hybrid-RAG (OpenAI embeddings + pgvector + 40+ payer schema knowledge graph), and LLM evaluation frameworks. He also built an MCP-native clinical data platform architecture, directly relevant for enterprise customers navigating the audit and governance requirements the Industries segment cares most about.

industryVertical.rationale: The Applied AI Architect, Industries role targets large enterprise buyers across verticals, not a single industry. The ICP is the enterprise procurement stakeholder: CIO, VP Engineering, AI Center of Excellence lead. Dan has deep experience navigating exactly this buyer in a hard vertical (healthcare RCM), which requires procurement approval chains, compliance review, and multi-stakeholder consensus. His C-suite workshop experience at Smarter Technologies maps directly. The gap is that he has not yet operated in industries outside healthcare at the enterprise level, which is minor for a horizontal role.

logistics.rationale: Dan is a confirmed SF resident (Richmond neighborhood, since January 2020) and the role is SF-based hybrid. The compensation band comfortably spans senior IC and director-level SF market rates. Regular travel aligns with his documented enterprise engagement history. No logistical blockers.

summary: 22-skill, 5-agent Cowork platform deployed live on $31.8M in enterprise pipeline
96% proposal conversion rate as forward-deployed pre-sales technical owner at Thoughtful AI
Only non-Product/Engineering person retained after 85% headcount reduction, specifically for AI legibility
MCP-native clinical data platform: 6 months of enterprise AI governance and audit architecture design
Automated field-to-engineering feedback pipeline using pre/post tool-use hooks and Notion integration

strengths.title: Enterprise C-suite AI adoption is documented and recent
strengths.description: At Smarter Technologies, I designed and ran discovery workshops with CFOs, CIOs, and operations leaders at multi-site healthcare organizations. These were not PowerPoint sessions: they were structured technical conversations that surfaced the real adoption blockers (compliance, data governance, workflow fit) and produced specific implementation roadmaps. That is exactly the Applied AI Architect motion for Industries customers.

strengths.title: Production Claude Code platform with embedded enablement infrastructure
strengths.description: Built a 22-skill, 5-agent Cowork platform with pre/post tool-use hooks and org-level governance, deployed live on $31.8M in enterprise pipeline. The platform was designed so non-technical sales reps could use it without writing a single prompt. That is the enterprise AI adoption problem Applied AI Architects solve at scale.

strengths.title: MCP-native enterprise AI governance design
strengths.description: Spent six months designing an MCP-native clinical data platform: explicit, auditable boundaries between the agent and external systems, which is exactly what enterprise IT approval chains need. The architecture question enterprises ask most is not 'can Claude do this' but 'can we control and audit what Claude does.' I have documented answers to that.

strengths.title: Only non-Product/Engineering person retained after 85% reduction
strengths.description: When Smarter Technologies shut down, I was the only person without a Product or Engineering title among the 15% retained. The PE firm made a specific exception. That happened because I can take genuinely complex AI systems and make them legible to the people who need to adopt them. That is the core Applied AI Architect value proposition.

weaknesses.title: Enterprise advisory has been internal, not on behalf of a vendor
weaknesses.description: My forward-deployed AI advisory work was within my own GTM org (advising prospects on Smarter Technologies AI) rather than advising enterprise customers on behalf of Anthropic as their AI vendor. The motion is similar but the credibility framing is different.
weaknesses.mitigation: The underlying skill is identical. The positioning shift from 'I use Claude' to 'I represent Anthropic' is a venue change, not a capability gap. My production platform gives me firsthand authority that most vendor advisors don't have.

weaknesses.title: Vertical experience is concentrated in healthcare
weaknesses.description: My enterprise customer experience is almost entirely in healthcare RCM. The Industries role serves customers across verticals. I have not yet navigated enterprise AI adoption in financial services, manufacturing, or retail.
weaknesses.mitigation: Healthcare procurement is among the most regulated and complex enterprise buying environments. The skills transfer to other verticals directly. And a horizontal role benefits from someone who has gone deep in at least one vertical.

referralBlurb: I am referring Dan Mathieson for the Applied AI Architect, Industries role. Dan has been doing the core motion of this job for the last three years: C-suite AI adoption workshops, forward-deployed enterprise discovery, and production LLM system design in a hard vertical (healthcare RCM). He built a 22-skill, 5-agent Claude Code platform running on $31.8M in pipeline, spent six months on MCP-native enterprise AI governance architecture, and was retained through an 85% headcount reduction specifically because he can make complex AI legible to non-technical stakeholders. His enterprise advisory has been internal rather than vendor-facing, but the underlying skill is exactly what this role demands.

solutioningDecks.title: Eligibility Automation
solutioningDecks.description: A PE-backed behavioral health network with ~1,500 clinicians and ~300K lookups a month. End-to-end ownership: pre-sales feasibility, architecture, a data-loss crisis I fixed at the system level, then renewal and expansion. The customer champion ran his own ROI study: ~$650K annualized, 3 to 3.5:1 realistic.

solutioningDecks.title: Epic Prior-Auth and Appeals Automation
solutioningDecks.description: A large hospital system running Epic. The key architecture decision - flat-file routing instead of direct API integration - solved IT access constraints and front-line staff trust in a single move. The cleanest win of the three: clear technical rationale, measurable throughput improvement.

solutioningDecks.title: Agentic Denial Management via EHR Partnership
solutioningDecks.description: A platform partnership with a major EHR vendor, building denial management across their ~80 customer base. The platform had no native denial object - the design reconstructed denial encounters from source data, becoming the reusable foundation for every customer on the platform.

applicationQuestions.question: Why do you want to work at Anthropic? (We value this response highly - great answers are often 200-400 words.)
applicationQuestions.answer:
With this now being the fourth time I've applied to Anthropic in the last four years, the reason I persist in wanting to join the mission remains the same. Namely, I believe Anthropic's mission of safe and reliable AI deployment throughout the economy is of the utmost importance, and that importance continues to grow with each successive application of mine. Dario said it best across multiple media appearances, the best way to ensure safe AI is to lead from the front and give the rest of the space no option but to follow. Claude Code, Cowork, managed agents, and the Claude SDK are all a direct manifestation of that thesis coming to life, and I'd love to help share the benefits that Anthropic's products have had on my life, both in my career and personal life, with people across the country and all over the world.

In my work with customers, I'm fascinated most by the variety and frequency of ways I can create what I call "lightbulb" moments. Often these moments come during demos of voice agents, or screen recordings of browser agents. More often than not these moments come from teams reporting increased job satisfaction. I try to create as many lightbulb moments as I can both for customers as well as my teammates internally. In fact, even after Thoughtful AI shut down, I still get messages from my coworkers asking to help them build out Claude Code-based personal knowledge systems for themselves. It's these moments of wonder that continue to prove that leading from the front is the right way to achieve the mission. I know I can help achieve the mission as an Applied AI Architect helping even more people leverage this technology for the benefit of all humanity.

applicationQuestions.question: This is a technical, client facing role. Do you have previous experience working with clients? Please describe your client-facing experience.
applicationQuestions.answer:
My last three roles have been entirely client-facing, and the through-line across all of them is the same: I own the technical relationship from initial pre-sales contact through production deployment and change management.

At Thoughtful AI I started as a Solutions Architect and quickly became the primary pre-sales technical voice. I built the company's first customer-facing analytics product, which became the core GTM artifact that contributed to our company's 3x YoY growth. Observability and demonstrating value from the customer's perspective goes a long way in enterprise AI adoption. 96% of the prospects I met with moved to proposal within a month. I delivered the company's first production GenAI system directly to a customer, ran 8-20 hour on-site SOP capture sessions at enterprise health systems, and owned everything from scoping through production sign-off.

At Smarter Technologies I built the full Pre-Sales Solutions Engineering function from scratch as Director of SE. I managed 61 simultaneous enterprise healthcare deals personally, ran structured discovery workshops with CFOs, CIOs, and operations leaders, delivered live technical architecture walkthroughs to CISOs and IT security leadership, and closed >$3M in revenue. I was simultaneously building the AI platform those customer conversations were about: a multi-agent system on Claude Code running on $31.8M in active pipeline.

At AccessHealthcare I was promoted to VP of Solutions specifically because of that client-facing track record. I was one of just 15% of the company retained through massive layoffs, and the only team member not explicitly in product and engineering at the time. I was retained specifically because of my ability to help customers understand how AI can help them achieve their goals. I now lead enterprise AI adoption strategy with external clients and run internal enablement across India-based delivery teams daily.

The consistent pattern: I am the person who gets put in front of the most technical, most skeptical, or highest-stakes customer conversations. I build trust by connecting the technology to the specific problem the customer is actually trying to solve, not the product's capabilities in the abstract. I build credibility by building systems customers can see and feel, and help them understand where AI both can and cannot help them achieve the ROI they want to achieve.

applicationQuestions.question: Please describe your experience working on any personal or professional projects that make use of large language models to create complex or interactive functionality.
applicationQuestions.answer:
The most substantial project is the GTM platform I built at Smarter Technologies. This platform is a 22-skill, 5-agent system on Claude Code that served as the primary operating system for managing $31.8M in enterprise pipeline. Each skill was built around a documented SOP that my team maintained and iterated on daily. We'd use skills and associated sub-agents to generate complete prospect-specific demo packages from structured context, apply current product roadmap data to every discovery call in real time, wait for the engineering team's weekly standup pages to be created in Notion and auto-populate a market feedback section from that week's field observations, and route each prospect touchpoint through a panel of sub-agent experts, each analyzing the interaction from a distinct stakeholder angle: technical evaluator, economic buyer, and champion. I implemented pre- and post-tool-use hooks to log every invocation and sample feedback on 15-20% of usages, achieving a 93% tool approval rating in the first three weeks of deployment. I then packaged the highest-impact skills into Cowork plugins and deployed them org-wide across sales, GTM, and delivery teams with no user-level prompt-writing required.

At Thoughtful AI I built the company's first production GenAI system: a hybrid-RAG eligibility verification solution that matched unstructured free-text coverage limitations to correct billing determinations using OpenAI embeddings and in-context learning. It hit 95% accuracy within two days on live billing workflows. I also built the eval infrastructure including labeled holdout datasets, precision/recall tracking by failure category, and regression test suites that ran before every model or prompt change.

On the personal side, my website runs a production agent built on the Anthropic SDK with tool use, streaming responses, and a RAG pipeline grounded in my career content. It is live at danielmathieson.com. I also use Claude Code daily as my primary development environment, with custom hooks and workflows I have built and iterated on over the past year.

You can navigate to my personal website listed above to interact with demo versions of the projects listed above. You can also navigate to https://www.danielmathieson.com/refer/f3a8c1d94e72 to view pre-loaded analyses of various roles my Claude SDK-based agent identified that I'd be a good fit for based on a substantial amount of career content I provided to the agent. Each role is scored across 5 dimensions for fit. You can also review all of this application content and more on the referral page specifically created for this role here: https://www.danielmathieson.com/refer/c3d5e7f9a246.
